option.value: {"/home/amirgm/IdeaProjects/comet":"{\"/src/main/java/dev/comet/security_context/domain/repository/UserRepository.java\":\"This file defines an interface called `UserRepository` that provides methods for managing user-related operations in a security context application.\\n\\n`UserRepository`: An interface that defines methods for managing user-related operations, such as saving, deleting, and retrieving users.\\n\\n`save(User user)`: Saves a new user to the repository.\\n`delete(Long id, String businessName)`: Deletes a user from the repository based on the provided ID and business name.\\n`findMembersById(Long id, String businessName)`: Retrieves a user from the repository based on the provided ID and business name.\\n`findMembersByUserName(String userName, String businessName)`: Retrieves a user from the repository based on the provided username and business name.\\n`existsByUserName(String userName)`: Checks if a user with the provided username already exists in the repository.\\n`checkIsAdmin(Role roleAdmin)`: Checks if the provided role is an admin role.\",\"/src/test/java/dev/comet/ModularityTest.java\":\"This file does not contain any classes or functions. Instead, it is a test class that verifies the modularity of the `CometApplication` class using the `ApplicationModules` utility provided by the Spring Modulith framework. The test class creates an instance of `ApplicationModules` using the `CometApplication` class as the entry point and then calls the `verify()` method to ensure that the application is properly modularized.\",\"/src/main/java/dev/comet/security_context/domain/value_object/user/FullName.java\":\"This file defines a `FullName` value object, which represents a person\\u0027s full name.\\n\\n`FullName`: A value object that represents a person\\u0027s full name, consisting of a first name and a last name.\\n\\n`of`: A static factory method that creates a new `FullName` instance from the provided first and last name.\\n\\n`validateName`: A private method that validates the provided name, ensuring it is not empty, is between 2 and 50 characters long, and contains only valid characters.\\n\\n`getAtomicValues`: An overridden method from the `ValueObject` class that returns the atomic values (first name and last name) that make up the `FullName` object.\",\"/src/main/java/dev/comet/security_context/application/dto/request/client/TokenRequestDto.java\":\"This file does not contain any classes or functions. Instead, it defines a record type called `TokenRequestDto` that represents the standard OAuth 2.1 token request parameters. The record includes fields for the grant type, client ID, client secret, scope, username, password, refresh token, authorization code, and redirect URI. This DTO is likely used in the application\\u0027s OAuth 2.1 token request handling logic.\",\"/src/main/java/dev/comet/security_context/domain/repository/RegisteredClientEntityRepository.java\":\"This file contains a single interface `RegisteredClientEntityRepository` which extends the `JpaRepository` interface provided by Spring Data JPA.\\n\\n`RegisteredClientEntityRepository`: This interface provides a way to interact with the `RegisteredClientEntity` entity in the database, including methods to find an entity by its `clientId`.\\n\\nThe file does not contain any classes or functions, but rather defines a repository interface that can be used to perform CRUD operations on the `RegisteredClientEntity` entity in the database.\",\"/src/main/java/dev/comet/security_context/application/dto/response/business/BusinessResponseDto.java\":\"This file contains a single class:\\n\\n`BusinessResponseDto`: A record class that represents the response data for a business, including its ID, name, members, activation status, and invitation code.\\n\\nThis file does not contain any functions. It is a data transfer object (DTO) that is used to transfer business-related data between different parts of the application.\",\"/src/main/java/dev/comet/security_context/infrastructure/web/rest/UserController.java\":\"This file contains a single class, `UserController`, which is a REST controller for user management endpoints. The file follows the DDD (Domain-Driven Design) and Modular Monolith architecture patterns.\\n\\n`UserController`: A REST controller for user management endpoints.\\n\\nThe file does not contain any functions, as it is a basic controller class that likely contains methods for handling user-related HTTP requests, such as creating, updating, and retrieving user data.\",\"/src/main/java/dev/comet/security_context/infrastructure/persistance/repository/JpaRegisteredClientRepository.java\":\"This file contains a single class and several functions:\\n\\n`JpaRegisteredClientRepository`: A Spring-based repository implementation for managing registered OAuth2 clients.\\n\\n`save(RegisteredClient registeredClient)`: Saves a registered client to the database using the provided mapper.\\n\\n`findById(String id)`: Retrieves a registered client by its unique identifier.\\n\\n`findByClientId(String clientId)`: Retrieves a registered client by its client ID.\\n\\n`toRegisteredClient(RegisteredClientEntity entity)`: Converts a database entity to a `RegisteredClient` object.\\n\\n`clientSetting(RegisteredClientEntity entity)`: Builds `ClientSettings` from the provided registered client entity.\\n\\n`tokenSettings()`: Constructs default `TokenSettings` for the registered clients.\\n\\nThis file implements the `RegisteredClientRepository` interface, which is part of the Spring Security OAuth2 Authorization Server. It provides a way to persist and retrieve registered OAuth2 clients using a JPA-based repository.\",\"/src/main/java/dev/comet/CometApplication.java\":\"This file is the main application class for a Spring Boot application called \\\"CometApplication\\\". It is responsible for bootstrapping the application and enabling caching and service discovery functionality.\\n\\n`CometApplication`: This is the main application class that is annotated with `@SpringBootApplication`, `@EnableCaching`, and `@EnableDiscoveryClient`, which enables Spring Boot auto-configuration, caching, and service discovery, respectively.\\n\\n`main(String[] args)`: This is the main entry point of the application, which calls the `SpringApplication.run()` method to start the Spring Boot application.\",\"/src/main/java/dev/comet/shared_context/building_block/BaseEntity.java\":\"This file contains a single abstract class:\\n\\n`BaseEntity\\u003cTId\\u003e`: An abstract class that represents a base entity with a unique identifier of type `TId`.\\n\\nThe file also contains the following functions:\\n\\n`BaseEntity(TId id)`: A constructor that initializes the `id` field of the `BaseEntity` class.\\n\\n`equals(Object other)`: A method that compares the current `BaseEntity` instance with another object to determine if they are equal based on their `id` values.\\n\\n`hashCode()`: A method that returns the hash code of the `id` field, which is used for efficient storage and retrieval of `BaseEntity` instances in collections.\\n\\nThis file provides a basic implementation of a base entity class that can be extended by other domain-specific entities, ensuring consistent handling of unique identifiers and equality/hash code comparisons.\",\"/src/main/java/dev/comet/security_context/application/dto/response/client/TokenResponseDto.java\":\"This file contains a single class:\\n\\n`TokenResponseDto`: Represents the standard OAuth 2.1 token response.\\n\\nThis file does not contain any functions. The purpose of this file is to define a data transfer object (DTO) for the OAuth 2.1 token response, which includes fields such as the access token, token type, expiration time, refresh token, and scope.\",\"/src/main/java/dev/comet/security_context/application/dto/request/user/UpdateUserProfileRequestDto.java\":\"This file does not contain any classes or functions. It defines a record type called `UpdateUserProfileRequestDto` which is a data transfer object (DTO) used for updating a user\\u0027s profile information. The DTO includes fields for the user\\u0027s full name, email, phone number, and office phone number, with validation constraints applied to each field.\",\"/src/main/java/dev/comet/security_context/application/service/SecurityContextService.java\":\"This file contains a single class and one function:\\n\\n`SecurityContextService`: A service class that provides functionality to retrieve the current user\\u0027s business ID from the security context.\\n\\n`getCurrentUserBusinessId()`: A function that retrieves the current user\\u0027s business ID from the security context and returns it as a `BusinessId` object.\\n\\nThe purpose of this file is to provide a service that can be used to access the current user\\u0027s business ID from the security context, which is likely used for authorization or other security-related purposes within the application.\",\"/src/main/java/dev/comet/security_context/application/service/user/UserServiceImpl.java\":\"This file contains a single class, `UserServiceImpl`, which is a service implementation for managing user-related operations in a security context application.\\n\\n`UserServiceImpl`: This class is responsible for handling various user-related operations, such as finding all members, finding active members, creating new users, deactivating users, and updating user profiles.\\n\\n`findAllMembers`: This function retrieves a paginated list of all members based on the provided business name.\\n\\n`findAllActiveMembers`: This function retrieves a paginated list of all active members based on the provided activation status and business name.\\n\\n`createNewUser`: This function creates a new user based on the provided user request DTO.\\n\\n`deactivateUser`: This function deactivates a user with the given user ID and business name.\\n\\n`updateUserProfile`: This function updates the user profile for the given user ID based on the provided update user profile request DTO.\",\"/src/main/java/dev/comet/security_context/domain/exception/InvalidInvitationCode.java\":\"This file contains a single class:\\n\\n`InvalidInvitationCode`: This class represents an exception that is thrown when an invalid invitation code is provided.\\n\\nThe file does not contain any functions. It simply defines a custom exception class that can be used to handle cases where an invalid invitation code is encountered.\",\"/src/main/java/dev/comet/security_context/domain/value_object/client/ClientSecret.java\":\"This file defines a `ClientSecret` value object that represents a client secret for a security context. The client secret must adhere to a specific pattern, which is enforced in the constructor.\\n\\n`ClientSecret`: A value object that represents a client secret for a security context, ensuring that the secret meets a specific pattern.\",\"/src/main/java/dev/comet/security_context/infrastructure/config/oauth/OAuthConstants.java\":\"This file contains a utility class `OAuthConstants` that defines a constant for the Vault Transit key name used for OAuth JWT signing.\\n\\n`OAuthConstants`: A utility class that holds constants related to OAuth 2.1 configuration.\\n\\n`OAuthConstants()`: A private constructor to prevent instantiation of the `OAuthConstants` class, as it is a utility class.\\n\\n`VAULT_TRANSIT_KEY_NAME`: The name of the Vault Transit key used for OAuth JWT signing.\",\"/src/main/java/dev/comet/security_context/infrastructure/config/mapper/UserDtoMapper.java\":\"This file contains a single class and one function:\\n\\n`UserDtoMapper`: A service class that maps a `User` domain model to a `UserResponseDto` data transfer object.\\n\\n`apply`: A function that takes a `User` object and returns a `UserResponseDto` object, mapping the user\\u0027s email, username, full name, business ID, and business name.\\n\\nThe purpose of this file is to provide a mapping between the `User` domain model and the `UserResponseDto` data transfer object, which can be used to transfer user data between the application and the presentation layer.\",\"/src/main/java/dev/comet/security_context/infrastructure/exception/InvalidVaultPathException.java\":\"This file contains a single class:\\n\\n`InvalidVaultPathException`: A custom runtime exception that is thrown when an invalid vault path is encountered.\\n\\nThis file does not contain any functions. It simply defines a custom exception class to handle cases where an invalid vault path is provided.\",\"/src/main/java/dev/comet/security_context/domain/exception/UserNotFoundException.java\":\"This file contains a single class:\\n\\n`UserNotFoundException`: A custom exception class that represents the case where a user is not found.\\n\\nThis file defines a custom exception class `UserNotFoundException` that extends the `RuntimeException` class. This exception can be thrown when a user is not found in the application.\",\"/src/main/java/dev/comet/security_context/infrastructure/config/security/JwtRolesAuthoritiesConverter.java\":\"This file contains a single class:\\n\\n`JwtRolesAuthoritiesConverter`: A custom converter that extracts roles from the \\\"rules\\\" claim in a JWT and converts them to Spring Security authorities.\\n\\nThe file does not contain any functions, but the class has the following method:\\n\\n`convert(Jwt jwt)`: Extracts the \\\"rules\\\" claim from the provided JWT, converts the roles to Spring Security authorities, and returns the collection of authorities.\\n\\nThis file is responsible for handling the conversion of JWT roles to Spring Security authorities, which is necessary for properly authorizing users based on the roles stored in the JWT.\",\"/src/main/java/dev/comet/security_context/domain/value_object/user/Id.java\":\"This file defines a `ValueObject` called `Id` that represents a user\\u0027s unique identifier.\\n\\n`Id`: A value object that represents a user\\u0027s unique identifier.\\n\\n`of(Long id)`: A static factory method that creates a new `Id` instance with the given `id` value.\\n\\n`validateId(Long id)`: A private method that validates the given `id` value, ensuring it is greater than 0.\\n\\n`getAtomicValues()`: An implementation of the `ValueObject` interface, which returns a list containing the `value` of the `Id` object.\",\"/src/main/java/dev/comet/security_context/infrastructure/config/mapper/BusinessAggregateMapper.java\":\"This file contains a single class and one function:\\n\\n`BusinessAggregateMapper`: A service class that maps a `BusinessEntity` object to a `Business` domain object.\\n\\n`apply`: A function that takes a `BusinessEntity` object and creates a `Business` domain object using the data from the `BusinessEntity`.\\n\\nThe purpose of this file is to provide a mapping between the `BusinessEntity` object, which is likely used for persistence, and the `Business` domain object, which represents the business entity in the application\\u0027s domain model.\",\"/src/main/java/dev/comet/security_context/infrastructure/persistance/model/BusinessEntity.java\":\"This file defines a `BusinessEntity` class, which represents a business entity in the application\\u0027s data model. The class has several properties, including an ID, name, creation date, office phone number, enabled status, a set of associated users, and an invitation code.\\n\\n`BusinessEntity`: A class that represents a business entity in the application\\u0027s data model, with properties such as ID, name, creation date, office phone number, enabled status, associated users, and invitation code.\\n\\nThe file does not contain any functions, as it is a data model class defined using Lombok annotations for generating boilerplate code.\",\"/src/main/java/dev/comet/security_context/domain/model/User.java\":\"This file defines a `User` class and several methods related to user management. Here\\u0027s a summary of the classes and functions:\\n\\n`User`: A user entity that represents a user in the system, with properties such as email, password, username, full name, phone number, business name, and user status.\\n\\n`create`: A factory method that creates a new `User` instance with the provided parameters.\\n\\n`updateProfile`: Updates the user\\u0027s full name, email, and phone number, but only if the user is active.\\n\\n`changePassword`: Changes the user\\u0027s password, but only if the user is active.\\n\\n`activate`: Activates the user, but only if the user is not already active or deleted.\\n\\n`deactivate`: Deactivates the user, but only if the user is already active.\\n\\n`assignRoles`: Assigns a set of roles to the user.\\n\\n`ensureIsActive`: A private method that checks if the user is active and throws an exception if not.\",\"/src/main/java/dev/comet/security_context/domain/value_object/user/Email.java\":\"`Email`: A value object that represents an email address and ensures its validity.\\n\\n`of`: A static factory method that creates a new `Email` instance from the provided value.\\n\\n`validateEmail`: A private method that validates the provided email address, ensuring it is not null or empty, and that it matches the expected email pattern.\\n\\n`getAtomicValues`: An implementation of the `ValueObject` interface, which returns the atomic values that make up the `Email` object.\\n\\nThis file defines a `ValueObject` called `Email` that represents an email address. It provides a static factory method to create new instances of `Email`, and includes validation logic to ensure the email address is valid. The `Email` class is part of the `security_context` domain in the application.\",\"/src/main/java/dev/comet/security_context/domain/exception/UserOperationException.java\":\"This file contains a single class:\\n\\n`UserOperationException`: A custom runtime exception class that represents an error that occurred during a user operation.\\n\\nThe file does not contain any functions. It simply defines a custom exception class that can be used to handle errors related to user operations in the application.\",\"/src/main/java/dev/comet/security_context/domain/value_object/user/PhoneNumber.java\":\"This file defines a `PhoneNumber` value object, which represents a phone number in the E.164 format.\\n\\n`PhoneNumber`: A value object that represents a phone number in the E.164 format.\\n\\n`of(String value)`: A static factory method that creates a new `PhoneNumber` instance from the given string value.\\n\\n`validate(String phone)`: A private method that validates the given phone number string against the E.164 format.\\n\\n`getAtomicValues()`: An implementation of the `ValueObject` interface, which returns the atomic values that make up the `PhoneNumber` object.\\n\\n`toString()`: An implementation of the `toString()` method, which returns the phone number value as a string.\",\"/src/main/java/dev/comet/security_context/domain/enums/client/GrantType.java\":\"This file defines an enumeration called `GrantType` that represents the different types of OAuth 2.0 grant types. The file does not contain any classes or functions, but rather defines the following grant types:\\n\\n`GrantType`: An enumeration that represents the different types of OAuth 2.0 grant types, including `AUTHORIZATION_CODE`, `REFRESH_TOKEN`, `CLIENT_CREDENTIALS`, `PASSWORD`, and `IMPLICIT`.\",\"/src/main/java/dev/comet/security_context/domain/exception/InvalidFullNameException.java\":\"This file contains a single class:\\n\\n`InvalidFullNameException`: A custom runtime exception that is thrown when an invalid full name is encountered.\\n\\nThe file does not contain any functions. It simply defines a custom exception class to handle cases where an invalid full name is provided.\",\"/src/main/java/dev/comet/security_context/infrastructure/config/mapper/RegisterClientMapper.java\":\"This file contains a single class and one function:\\n\\n`RegisterClientMapper`: A service class that maps a `RegisteredClient` object to a `RegisteredClientEntity` object.\\n\\n`apply`: A function that takes a `RegisteredClient` object and returns a `RegisteredClientEntity` object with the corresponding properties.\\n\\nThe purpose of this file is to provide a mapping between the `RegisteredClient` object, which is used in the Spring Security OAuth2 server, and the `RegisteredClientEntity` object, which is likely used for persistence in the application\\u0027s infrastructure.\",\"/src/main/java/dev/comet/security_context/application/dto/request/client/ClientRequestDto.java\":\"This file contains a single class:\\n\\n`ClientRequestDto`: A record class that represents a request DTO for a client, containing the client\\u0027s name, ID, and secret.\\n\\nThis file does not contain any functions. It serves as a data transfer object (DTO) for client-related requests in the security context application.\",\"/src/main/java/dev/comet/security_context/infrastructure/exception/TransitKeyValidationException.java\":\"This file defines a custom exception class `TransitKeyValidationException` that extends `RuntimeException` and is used to handle errors related to the validation of a transit key.\\n\\n`TransitKeyValidationException`: A custom exception class that represents an error during the validation of a transit key.\\n\\n`TransitKeyValidationException(String message)`: A constructor that creates a new `TransitKeyValidationException` instance with the provided error message.\\n\\n`TransitKeyValidationException(String message, Throwable cause)`: A constructor that creates a new `TransitKeyValidationException` instance with the provided error message and the underlying cause of the exception.\",\"/src/main/java/dev/comet/security_context/domain/repository/package-info.java\":\"This file does not contain any classes or functions. It appears to be a package declaration for the \\\"domain-repository-layer\\\" module in the \\\"dev.comet.security_context.domain.repository\\\" package, which is likely part of a larger Spring Modulith application.\",\"/src/main/java/dev/comet/security_context/domain/value_object/user/Password.java\":\"`Password`: A value object that represents a user\\u0027s password and enforces password validation rules.\\n\\n`of`: A static factory method that creates a new `Password` instance from a given string value.\\n\\n`validatePassword`: A private method that validates the password string against a set of rules, throwing an `InvalidPasswordException` if the password is invalid.\\n\\n`getAtomicValues`: An abstract method from the `ValueObject` interface that is not implemented in this class.\\n\\nThis file defines a `Password` value object that is responsible for validating and representing a user\\u0027s password. It enforces a set of password rules, such as minimum length, inclusion of uppercase and lowercase letters, numbers, and special characters. The `Password` class is part of the security context domain and is designed to be used as a building block in a larger application.\",\"/src/main/java/dev/comet/security_context/application/dto/request/user/UserRequestDto.java\":\"This file contains a single class, `UserRequestDto`, which is a data transfer object (DTO) used for creating a new user by an Owner. The DTO includes fields for the user\\u0027s ID, username, password, full name, email, and phone number, with validation constraints applied to each field.\\n\\n`UserRequestDto`: A DTO class for creating a new user by an Owner, with validation constraints on the fields.\",\"/src/main/java/dev/comet/security_context/domain/enums/user/Role.java\":\"This file defines an enumeration called `Role` that represents the different roles a user can have in the application. The `Role` enum has two values: `OWNER` and `ADMIN`.\\n\\nThis file does not contain any classes or functions. It simply defines an enumeration that can be used to represent the different roles a user can have in the application.\",\"/src/main/java/dev/comet/security_context/application/dto/response/user/UserResponseDto.java\":\"This file contains a single class:\\n\\n`UserResponseDto`: A record class that represents the response data for a user, including their email, username, full name, business ID, business name, and user status.\\n\\nThis file does not contain any functions. It is a data transfer object (DTO) that is used to encapsulate and transfer user-related data between different layers of the application.\",\"/src/main/java/dev/comet/shared_context/building_block/Aggregate.java\":\"This file contains the following:\\n\\n`Aggregate\\u003cTId\\u003e`: An abstract class that represents an aggregate root in a domain-driven design (DDD) architecture, extending the `BaseEntity\\u003cTId\\u003e` class.\\n\\n`Aggregate(TId)`: The constructor for the `Aggregate\\u003cTId\\u003e` class, which takes an identifier of type `TId` and passes it to the constructor of the `BaseEntity\\u003cTId\\u003e` class.\\n\\nThe purpose of this file is to provide a base class for aggregate roots in a DDD-based application. Aggregate roots are the entry point for interacting with a domain model, and this class provides a common foundation for implementing them.\",\"/src/main/java/dev/comet/security_context/domain/exception/InvalidUserNameException.java\":\"This file contains a single class:\\n\\n`InvalidUserNameException`: This exception is thrown when a user\\u0027s username is invalid.\\n\\nThe file does not contain any functions. It simply defines a custom exception class that can be used to indicate that a user\\u0027s username is invalid.\",\"/src/main/java/dev/comet/security_context/domain/enums/user/UserStatus.java\":\"This file defines a Java enum called `UserStatus` that represents the possible status of a user, either `ACTIVE` or `NOT_ACTIVE`. The file does not contain any classes or functions, but rather defines a simple enumeration to represent the different user status values.\",\"/src/main/java/dev/comet/security_context/infrastructure/config/external/vault/VaultTransitJwtConfig.java\":\"This file contains a single class and several functions:\\n\\n`VaultTransitJwtConfig`: A configuration class that sets up a JwtEncoder and a JWKSource for signing and verifying JWT tokens using a Vault Transit key.\\n\\n`vaultTransitJwtEncoder()`: A bean method that returns a JwtEncoder implementation that uses the Vault Transit key to sign JWT tokens.\\n\\n`jwkSource(VaultOperations)`: A static bean method that creates a JWKSource implementation that retrieves the public key from the Vault Transit key and uses it to verify JWT tokens.\\n\\n`getPublicKeyObj(VaultResponse)`: A static helper method that extracts the public key from the Vault Transit key response.\\n\\n`encode(JwtEncoderParameters)`: An implementation of the JwtEncoder interface that signs a JWT token using the Vault Transit key.\\n\\n`createSigningInput(JwtEncoderParameters)`: A helper method that creates the signing input for the JWT token by encoding the header and claims.\\n\\n`signWithVault(String)`: A helper method that uses the Vault Transit key to sign the input string and returns the signature.\",\"/src/main/java/dev/comet/security_context/domain/value_object/business/InvitationCode.java\":\"This file contains a single class, `InvitationCode`, which is a value object that represents an invitation code. The class has a single function, `generateCode`, which generates the invitation code based on a `BusinessId`.\\n\\n`InvitationCode`: A value object that represents an invitation code, generated from a `BusinessId`.\\n\\n`generateCode`: Generates an invitation code by hashing the `BusinessId` and truncating the result to 16 characters.\",\"/src/main/java/dev/comet/shared_context/building_block/ValueObject.java\":\"This file defines a base class for Value Objects in a domain model. It provides a common implementation for equality comparison and hash code generation based on the atomic values of the Value Object.\\n\\n`ValueObject`: A base class for Value Objects in the domain model, which are immutable by design and enforce consistency and reliability across the system.\\n\\n`getAtomicValues()`: An abstract method that returns an `Iterable` of the atomic values that make up the Value Object.\\n\\n`equals(ValueObject)`: Compares two Value Objects based on their atomic values.\\n\\n`equals(Object)`: Overrides the default `equals` method to compare Value Objects based on their atomic values.\\n\\n`hashCode()`: Generates a hash code for the Value Object based on its atomic values.\\n\\n`valuesAreEqual(ValueObject)`: A private method that compares the atomic values of two Value Objects using a stream-based approach.\",\"/src/main/java/dev/comet/security_context/infrastructure/config/mapper/UserRequestMapper.java\":\"This file contains a mapper class that converts request DTOs to domain value objects.\\n\\n`UserRequestMapper`: A component class that maps user request DTOs to domain value objects.\\n\\n`toValueObjects(UserRequestDto request)`: Converts a `UserRequestDto` to a `UserValueObjects` instance.\\n\\n`toValueObjects(UpdateUserProfileRequestDto request)`: Converts an `UpdateUserProfileRequestDto` to a `UserProfileValueObjects` instance.\\n\\n`parseFullName(String fullName)`: Parses a full name string into a `FullName` value object.\\n\\n`UserValueObjects`: A record class representing the value objects for a user.\\n\\n`UserProfileValueObjects`: A record class representing the value objects for a user\\u0027s profile.\",\"/src/main/java/dev/comet/security_context/infrastructure/config/security/JpaUserDetailsService.java\":\"This file contains a single class and one function:\\n\\n`JpaUserDetailsService`: A service class that implements the `UserDetailsService` interface to load user details from a JPA repository.\\n\\n`loadUserByUsername`: A function that retrieves a user\\u0027s details from the JPA repository based on the provided username, or throws a `UsernameNotFoundException` if the user is not found.\\n\\nThis file is part of the security configuration for a Spring application, and it provides a custom implementation of the `UserDetailsService` interface to load user details from a JPA repository.\",\"/src/main/java/dev/comet/security_context/infrastructure/persistance/repository/user/UserJpaRepository.java\":\"This file contains a Spring Data JPA repository interface named `UserJpaRepository` that extends the `JpaRepository` interface. The repository provides methods for interacting with the `UserEntity` table in the database.\\n\\n`UserJpaRepository`: A Spring Data JPA repository interface for managing `UserEntity` objects.\\n\\n`findUserAndBusiness(String username)`: Retrieves a `UserEntity` and its associated `Business` entity by the user\\u0027s username.\\n\\n`findBusinessMembersWithId(Long id, String businessName)`: Retrieves a `UserEntity` by its ID and the associated business name.\\n\\n`findBusinessMembersWithUserName(String userName, String businessName)`: Retrieves a `UserEntity` by its username and the associated business name.\\n\\n`deleteBusinessMember(Long id, String businessName)`: Deletes a `UserEntity` by its ID and the associated business name.\\n\\nThe file also includes a commented-out section where the developer intended to create a method for finding users with the \\\"ADMIN\\\" role, but was unable to complete it due to an internet problem.\",\"/src/main/java/dev/comet/security_context/domain/model/Client.java\":\"`Client`: A class that represents a client in the security context, containing information such as client ID, name, secret, authentication methods, authorization grant types, redirect URIs, scopes, and consent requirement.\\n\\n`of`: A static factory method that creates a new `Client` instance with the provided client ID, name, and secret, and initializes the other properties with default values.\\n\\nThis file defines the `Client` class, which is an aggregate root in the security context domain. The `Client` class encapsulates the properties and behavior of a client, including its authentication methods, authorization grant types, redirect URIs, scopes, and consent requirement. The file also provides a static factory method `of` to create new `Client` instances.\",\"/src/main/java/dev/comet/security_context/domain/enums/client/ClientAuthMethod.java\":\"This file defines an enumeration `ClientAuthMethod` that represents the different authentication methods that a client can use to authenticate with the system. The enumeration has three values: `CLIENT_SECRET_BASIC`, `CLIENT_SECRET_POST`, and `NONE`.\\n\\nThis file does not contain any classes or functions. It simply defines an enumeration that can be used to represent the different client authentication methods in the application.\",\"/src/main/java/dev/comet/security_context/domain/exception/InvalidPasswordException.java\":\"This file contains a single class:\\n\\n`InvalidPasswordException`: This exception is thrown when an invalid password is provided.\\n\\nThe file defines a custom exception class `InvalidPasswordException` that extends `RuntimeException`. This exception can be used to indicate that an invalid password has been provided during the execution of the application.\",\"/src/main/java/dev/comet/security_context/domain/exception/InvalidPhoneNumberException.java\":\"This file contains a single class:\\n\\n`InvalidPhoneNumberException`: A custom runtime exception that is thrown when a phone number is invalid.\\n\\nThis file defines a custom exception class `InvalidPhoneNumberException` that extends `RuntimeException`. This exception can be used to indicate that a provided phone number is invalid.\",\"/src/main/java/dev/comet/security_context/infrastructure/config/security/oauth/AuthorizationServerConfiguration.java\":\"This file is a Spring Security configuration class that sets up an OAuth2 authorization server and a resource server for a web application.\\n\\n`AuthorizationServerConfiguration`: This class configures the OAuth2 authorization server and the resource server for the application.\\n\\n`authServer`: This function configures the OAuth2 authorization server, including setting up the OIDC endpoints and disabling CSRF protection.\\n\\n`api`: This function configures the resource server, including allowing access to the token endpoint and account creation endpoint, and requiring authentication for all other requests.\\n\\n`authorizationService`: This function creates an in-memory OAuth2 authorization service.\\n\\n`jwkSource`: This function creates a JWK source for the JWT tokens used by the authorization server.\\n\\n`jwtAuthenticationConverter`: This function creates a JWT authentication converter that extracts roles from the \\\"rules\\\" claim in the JWT token.\\n\\n`passwordEncoder`: This function creates a BCrypt password encoder for password hashing.\",\"/src/main/java/dev/comet/security_context/domain/value_object/user/OfficePhoneNumber.java\":\"`OfficePhoneNumber`: A value object that represents a valid Iranian office phone number.\\n\\n`of`: A factory method that creates an `OfficePhoneNumber` instance from a given string.\\n\\n`validate`: A private method that validates the input string to ensure it matches the expected Iranian office phone number format.\\n\\n`getFullNumber`: A method that returns the full office phone number.\\n\\n`getAtomicValues`: A method that returns the atomic values of the `OfficePhoneNumber` object.\\n\\nThis file defines a value object called `OfficePhoneNumber` that represents a valid Iranian office phone number. It includes methods to create, validate, and retrieve the phone number.\",\"/src/main/java/dev/comet/security_context/infrastructure/config/external/database/DatabaseCredentials.java\":\"This file contains a single record class, `DatabaseCredentials`, which represents the username and password for a database connection.\\n\\n`DatabaseCredentials`: A record class that holds the username and password for a database connection.\",\"/src/main/java/dev/comet/security_context/infrastructure/config/security/VaultSecurityValidator.java\":\"This file contains a single class, `VaultSecurityValidator`, which is responsible for validating the connectivity to Vault and the presence of the OAuth signing key at application startup. The class performs the following functions:\\n\\n`VaultSecurityValidator`: Validates Vault connectivity and OAuth signing key presence at application startup, and fails fast if security prerequisites are not met.\\n\\n`validateVaultSecurity()`: Validates Vault connectivity, signing key presence, and public key access, and logs the results.\\n\\n`validateVaultConnectivity()`: Validates the connectivity to Vault by attempting to read a known path.\\n\\n`validateSigningKeyPresence()`: Validates the presence of the Transit signing key in Vault.\\n\\n`validatePublicKeyAccess()`: Validates the ability to access the public key from the Transit signing key in Vault.\\n\\n`getPublicKeyObj()`: Extracts the public key object from the Vault response.\",\"/src/main/java/dev/comet/security_context/infrastructure/persistance/model/UserEntity.java\":\"This file contains a single class, `UserEntity`, which implements the `UserDetails` interface from the Spring Security framework.\\n\\n`UserEntity`: A JPA entity class that represents a user in the application, with various properties such as username, email, password, and roles.\\n\\n`mapRoles(Set\\u003cRole\\u003e roles)`: A private helper method that maps the user\\u0027s roles to a set of `GrantedAuthority` objects, which are used by Spring Security for authorization.\\n\\n`getAuthorities()`: An implementation of the `UserDetails` interface method that returns the user\\u0027s granted authorities (roles).\\n\\n`getPassword()`: An implementation of the `UserDetails` interface method that returns the user\\u0027s password.\\n\\n`getUsername()`: An implementation of the `UserDetails` interface method that returns the user\\u0027s username.\\n\\n`isAccountNonExpired()`: An implementation of the `UserDetails` interface method that always returns `true`, indicating that the user\\u0027s account is not expired.\\n\\n`isAccountNonLocked()`: An implementation of the `UserDetails` interface method that checks the user\\u0027s status and returns `true` if the user is not locked.\\n\\n`isCredentialsNonExpired()`: An implementation of the `UserDetails` interface method that always returns `true`, indicating that the user\\u0027s credentials are not expired.\\n\\n`isEnabled()`: An implementation of the `UserDetails` interface method that checks the user\\u0027s status and the enabled status of the associated business entity, and returns `true` if the user is active and the business is enabled.\",\"/src/main/java/dev/comet/security_context/application/service/user/UserService.java\":\"This file contains an interface named `UserService` that defines the application service for user management operations. The interface follows the Domain-Driven Design (DDD) pattern and orchestrates the domain operations related to user management.\\n\\n`UserService`: An interface that defines the application service for user management operations.\\n\\n`findAllMembers`: Retrieves all users in the authenticated user\\u0027s business, requiring the `OWNER` role.\\n\\n`findAllActiveMembers`: Retrieves all active users in the authenticated user\\u0027s business, requiring the `OWNER` role.\\n\\n`createNewUser`: Creates a new user in the authenticated user\\u0027s business, requiring the `OWNER` role.\\n\\n`deactivateUser`: Deletes a user by ID, requiring the `OWNER` role.\\n\\n`updateUserProfile`: Updates the user profile, requiring the `OWNER` role.\",\"/src/main/java/dev/comet/security_context/infrastructure/config/mapper/BusinessDtoMapper.java\":\"`BusinessDtoMapper`: A service class that maps a `Business` domain object to a `BusinessResponseDto` data transfer object.\\n\\n`apply`: A function that takes a `Business` object and returns a `BusinessResponseDto` object, mapping the business properties and its members.\\n\\nThis file contains a single class, `BusinessDtoMapper`, which is responsible for mapping a `Business` domain object to a `BusinessResponseDto` data transfer object. The `apply` function within the class performs this mapping, converting the business properties and its members to the corresponding DTO representation.\",\"/src/main/java/dev/comet/security_context/domain/exception/InvalidEmailException.java\":\"This file contains a single class:\\n\\n`InvalidEmailException`: A custom runtime exception that is thrown when an invalid email address is encountered.\\n\\nThis file defines a custom exception class `InvalidEmailException` that extends the `RuntimeException` class. This exception can be used to indicate that an invalid email address has been encountered during the execution of the application.\",\"/src/main/java/dev/comet/security_context/infrastructure/persistance/repository/business/BusinessJpaRepository.java\":\"This file contains a single interface, `BusinessJpaRepository`, which is a Spring Data JPA repository for the `BusinessEntity` model. The interface extends the `JpaRepository` interface, which provides basic CRUD (Create, Read, Update, Delete) operations for the `BusinessEntity` entity, identified by a `UUID` primary key.\\n\\n`BusinessJpaRepository`: An interface that provides a set of methods for interacting with the `BusinessEntity` model in the database.\",\"/src/main/java/dev/comet/security_context/infrastructure/config/mapper/UserAggregateMapper.java\":\"This file contains a single class and one function:\\n\\n`UserAggregateMapper`: A service class that maps a `UserEntity` object to a `User` domain object.\\n\\n`apply`: A function that takes a `UserEntity` object and returns a `User` domain object, mapping the entity\\u0027s properties to the corresponding value objects in the domain model.\\n\\nThe purpose of this file is to provide a mapping between the persistence layer\\u0027s `UserEntity` and the domain model\\u0027s `User` object, allowing the application to work with the domain-specific representation of a user.\",\"/src/main/java/dev/comet/security_context/application/dto/request/business/BusinessRequestDto.java\":\"This file contains a single class:\\n\\n`BusinessRequestDto`: A data transfer object for creating a new business, which includes the business name, a list of business members, and an invitation code.\\n\\nThis file does not contain any functions. It is a data transfer object class used to encapsulate the necessary information for creating a new business in the application.\",\"/src/main/java/dev/comet/security_context/infrastructure/config/security/oauth/TokenCustomizerConfig.java\":\"This file is a Spring Security configuration class that customizes the JWT token generation process for an OAuth2 application.\\n\\n`TokenCustomizerConfig`: This class configures a `OAuth2TokenCustomizer` bean that adds additional claims to the generated JWT tokens.\\n\\n`tokenCustomizer()`: This function is a bean method that returns the `OAuth2TokenCustomizer` implementation. It adds the following claims to the JWT token: `client_id`, `username`, `business_id`, `rules` (user authorities), and `scope`.\",\"/src/main/java/dev/comet/security_context/infrastructure/persistance/repository/user/UserRepositoryImpl.java\":\"This file contains a single class `UserRepositoryImpl` which implements the `UserRepository` interface. The file also contains several functions that provide CRUD (Create, Read, Update, Delete) operations for managing user data.\\n\\n`UserRepositoryImpl`: A repository implementation that handles user-related operations, such as saving, deleting, and finding users.\\n\\n`save(User user)`: Saves a user to the repository.\\n`delete(Long id, String businessName)`: Deletes a user from the repository based on their ID and business name.\\n`findMembersById(Long id, String businessName)`: Finds a user by their ID and business name.\\n`findMembersByUserName(String userName, String businessName)`: Finds a user by their username and business name.\\n`existsByUserName(String userName)`: Checks if a user with the given username exists.\\n`checkIsAdmin(Role roleAdmin)`: Checks if a user has the admin role (not implemented correctly).\",\"/src/main/java/dev/comet/security_context/domain/model/Business.java\":\"This file defines a `Business` class, which represents a business entity in the security context domain. The class has several value objects as properties, such as `BusinessName`, `OfficePhoneNumber`, and `InvitationCode`. The file also includes two factory methods, `create` and `showAllMembers`, which are used to create a new `Business` instance and to retrieve all members of the business, respectively.\\n\\n`Business`: A class that represents a business entity in the security context domain.\\n\\n`create`: A factory method that creates a new `Business` instance with the provided `BusinessName`, `OfficePhoneNumber`, and `BusinessId`.\\n\\n`showAllMembers`: A domain invariant method that retrieves all members of the business.\\n\\n`addNewMember`: A domain invariant method that adds a new member to the business.\",\"/src/main/java/dev/comet/security_context/infrastructure/persistance/model/RegisteredClientEntity.java\":\"This file contains a single class, `RegisteredClientEntity`, which is an entity class used for storing information about registered OAuth clients in a database.\\n\\n`RegisteredClientEntity`: An entity class that represents a registered OAuth client, storing information such as the client ID, client name, client secret, client authentication methods, authorization grant types, redirect URIs, scopes, and consent requirements.\\n\\nThe file does not contain any functions, but rather defines the structure and properties of the `RegisteredClientEntity` class, which is likely used in the context of an OAuth2 implementation within a Spring Security-based application.\",\"/src/main/java/dev/comet/security_context/domain/value_object/business/BusinessId.java\":\"This file defines a `BusinessId` class, which is a Value Object for the Business Aggregate in the Security Context domain. It represents a unique identifier for a business entity.\\n\\n`BusinessId`: A Value Object that represents a unique identifier for a business entity.\\n\\n`BusinessId(UUID value)`: Constructs a `BusinessId` instance with the provided UUID value.\\n\\n`generate()`: Generates a new `BusinessId` instance with a randomly generated UUID.\\n\\n`fromString(String uuidString)`: Constructs a `BusinessId` instance from a given UUID string.\\n\\n`equals(Object o)`: Compares two `BusinessId` instances for equality.\\n\\n`hashCode()`: Generates a hash code for a `BusinessId` instance.\\n\\n`getAtomicValues()`: Returns the atomic values that make up the `BusinessId` instance.\",\"/src/main/java/dev/comet/security_context/domain/value_object/client/ClientId.java\":\"This file defines a `ClientId` value object, which represents a unique identifier for a client.\\n\\n`ClientId`: A value object that represents a unique identifier for a client.\\n\\n`ClientId(UUID value)`: Constructs a `ClientId` instance with the provided `UUID` value, ensuring that the value is not null.\\n\\n`ClientId(String value)`: Constructs a `ClientId` instance from a string representation of a `UUID`.\\n\\n`generate()`: Generates a new `ClientId` instance with a randomly generated `UUID`.\",\"/src/main/java/dev/comet/shared_context/package-info.java\":\"This file does not contain any classes or functions. It is an application module declaration for the `dev.comet.shared_context` package, which is marked as an `OPEN` type application module using the `@ApplicationModule` annotation provided by the Spring Modulith framework. This indicates that this module is intended to be used and accessed by other modules within the application.\",\"/src/main/java/dev/comet/security_context/infrastructure/config/security/MethodSecurityConfig.java\":\"This file does not contain any classes or functions. Instead, it is a configuration class that enables method-level security annotations in a Spring Security application.\\n\\nThe file configures the `MethodSecurityConfig` class, which enables the use of `@PreAuthorize`, `@PostAuthorize`, `@PreFilter`, and `@PostFilter` annotations in the application. These annotations allow for fine-grained access control at the method level, enabling developers to specify security requirements for individual methods.\",\"/src/main/java/dev/comet/security_context/domain/exception/BusinessNotFoundException.java\":\"This file contains a single class:\\n\\n`BusinessNotFoundException`: A custom runtime exception that is thrown when a business is not found.\\n\\nThis file defines a custom runtime exception called `BusinessNotFoundException` that can be used to indicate that a business was not found during an operation. The exception takes a message string as a parameter in its constructor, which can be used to provide more information about the specific error that occurred.\",\"/src/main/java/dev/comet/security_context/domain/exception/IllegalActivationStatus.java\":\"This file contains a single class:\\n\\n`IllegalActivationStatus`: A custom runtime exception that represents an illegal activation status.\\n\\nThe file does not contain any functions. It simply defines a custom exception class that can be used to indicate an illegal activation status in the application.\",\"/src/main/java/dev/comet/security_context/infrastructure/config/external/database/DatabaseSecretService.java\":\"`DatabaseSecretService`: A service class that retrieves database credentials from a Vault server.\\n\\n`getDatabaseCredentials()`: Retrieves the database password from the Vault server using the configured Vault path, and returns it as a string.\\n\\nThis file contains a single service class, `DatabaseSecretService`, which is responsible for retrieving database credentials from a Vault server. The class uses the `VaultTemplate` provided by the Spring Vault integration to read the database credentials from the configured Vault path, and returns the password as a string. If the credentials are not found in the Vault, an `InvalidVaultPathException` is thrown.\",\"/src/main/java/dev/comet/security_context/application/service/business/BusinessService.java\":\"This file does not contain any classes, but it does contain the following interface:\\n\\n`BusinessService`: This interface defines the contract for a service that handles the creation of new business accounts.\\n\\n`createNewAccount`: This function creates a new business account based on the provided `UserRequestDto` and returns a `BusinessResponseDto` containing the created account information. This is a public endpoint that does not require authentication.\",\"/src/main/java/dev/comet/security_context/domain/repository/BusinessRepository.java\":\"This file defines an interface called `BusinessRepository` that provides a method for retrieving a `Business` object by its unique identifier (`UUID`).\\n\\n`BusinessRepository`: An interface that defines a repository for managing `Business` entities.\\n\\n`findById(UUID businessId)`: A method that retrieves a `Business` entity by its unique identifier (`UUID`), returning an `Optional` to handle the case where the entity is not found.\",\"/src/main/java/dev/comet/security_context/domain/value_object/client/ClientName.java\":\"This file defines a `ClientName` value object, which represents the name of a client.\\n\\n`ClientName`: A value object that represents the name of a client, ensuring that the name is not empty.\",\"/src/main/java/dev/comet/security_context/infrastructure/persistance/repository/business/BusinessRepositoryImpl.java\":\"This file contains a single class and one function:\\n\\n`BusinessRepositoryImpl`: This class is an implementation of the `BusinessRepository` interface, which provides a way to interact with the business data in the application.\\n\\n`findById`: This function retrieves a `Business` entity from the database based on the provided `businessId`.\\n\\nThe file is responsible for implementing the `BusinessRepository` interface, which is used to interact with the business data in the application.
